description_long: This object is a collection of three grave markers, including two large Celtic-style stone crosses and a traditional rectangular headstone, all set on rectangular stone bases. The crosses are weathered, with one showing a broken arm and the other featuring a slightly tilted shaft, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The rectangular headstone is simple and unadorned, positioned between the two crosses. One of the crosses is accompanied by a stone slab, possibly representing a grave cover or sarcophagus, with a raised section at the head. The surfaces of all elements are rough and dark gray, resembling aged stone, with some greenish discoloration indicating moss or lichen growth. No visible inscriptions or engravings are present on any of the grave markers.
description: A set of three weathered stone grave markers, including two large Celtic crosses and a simple headstone, all on rectangular bases with a dark, aged appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing two large Celtic crosses, a central rectangular headstone, and a stone slab with a raised section at the head.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the crosses and the stone slab, with the headstone appearing as a thin vertical element.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the crosses and headstone, with the broken arm of one cross and the stone slab clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the profile of the stone slab and the alignment of the grave markers.
